objective: Create internal/git/log.go — three thin history-query methods over the shipped, unexported (g *Git) run(args ...string) exec seam against the REAL git binary (PRD §22.3 shell-out, no go-git; §19 no sh -c): (1) CommitCount() (int, error) running `git rev-list --count HEAD` and returning 0 (NO error) on an unborn/rootless repo; (2) RecentMessages(n int) (string, error) running `git log --format=---%n%B -<n>` and returning the RAW output verbatim (the prompt layer trims blanks + caps lines; do NOT trim here), empty string (NO error) on an unborn repo; (3) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error) running `git log --format=%s -<n>`, splitting into trimmed lines newest-first, empty slice (NO error) on an unborn repo. Plus internal/git/log_test.go (white-box package git, stdlib testing ONLY, REAL git binary via the shipped S2 harness newTempRepo/seedCommits) covering the contract MOCKING scenarios: counts match a seeded history, subjects returned newest-first in order (and truncated to the n newest), raw `---`-separated multi-line bodies preserved untrimmed, and an unborn repo returns 0 / empty for all three methods. This supplies the prompt-example history (P1.M4.T1.S1), the multi-line detect input (reference_impl.md §6), and the dedupe subject set (P1.M6.T1.S2).
context:
# PRP — P1.M3.T4.S1: internal/git/log.go — CommitCount / RecentMessages / RecentSubjects

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Add three history-query primitives to the EXISTING `package git`, all thin methods over the shipped, unexported `(g *Git) run(args ...string)` exec seam against the REAL git binary (no go-git, no sh -c): `CommitCount() (int, error)`, `RecentMessages(n int) (string, error)`, and `RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)`. These faithfully port the proven `commit-pi` history reads (reference_impl.md §1 + §6; external_deps.md §D): the commit count (`git rev-list --count HEAD || 0`), the example messages for prompt assembly + multi-line detection (`git log --format='---%n%B' -20`), and the duplicate-subject set (`git log --format=%s -50`).

**Deliverable**: TWO NEW files in the EXISTING `internal/git` package:
- `internal/git/log.go` — `func (g *Git) CommitCount() (int, error)`, `func (g *Git) RecentMessages(n int) (string, error)`, `func (g *Git) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)`. Each builds a `[]string` of args, calls `g.run`, and routes a non-zero exit via `errors.As` into the typed `*ExitError`. Uses stdlib ONLY (`errors`, `fmt`, `strconv`, `strings`). Plain `package git` line (git.go OWNS the `// Package git` doc — do NOT duplicate it).
- `internal/git/log_test.go` — white-box `package git`, stdlib `testing` ONLY, drives the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0) via the shipped S2 harness (`newTempRepo` / `seedCommits` in gittestutil_test.go), one behavior per `Test*`.

**Success Definition**: `go build ./internal/git/`, `go vet ./internal/git/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/git/` empty; `go test ./internal/git/` PASS (the log scenarios + existing S1/S2/T2/T3 tests unaffected); `go test ./...` whole-module green. On an unborn repo all three methods return the empty result (0 / "" / nil) with a NIL error (the normal root-commit case, NOT a failure); a seeded linear history yields an exact count, subjects newest-first in order, and the raw `---`-separated `%B` message bodies (blank lines intact, NOT trimmed). stdlib-only imports; no go.mod/go.sum change; the shipped git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/plumbing*.go/diff*.go, main.go, Makefile, internal/ui, internal/provider untouched; no `go mod tidy`; `// Package git` doc appears exactly once (in git.go).

## Why

- **History is the style-learning signal.** The generate step only builds example-backed prompts when there IS a style to learn. `CommitCount()` (>1 ⇒ build examples) gates that, `RecentMessages(20)` feeds the prompt's example block AND the multi-line-detect awk heuristic (reference_impl.md §6), and `RecentSubjects(50)` is the anti-duplicate set (reference_impl.md §1 / external_deps.md §D). Getting these three reads byte-faithful to the proven reference is what makes the generated message match the repo's style and never duplicate an existing subject.
- **The unborn/root repo is the NORMAL first-run case, not an error.** On a fresh repo `git rev-list --count HEAD` and `git log` both exit 128. The proven reference swallows this (`|| 0`) and the contract pins "root repo → 0 / empty" with NO error. plumbing.go's `RevParseHEAD` already establishes the package's `errors.As(&ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, ...)` detection pattern for the unborn case; log.go mirrors it (with the `git log`-specific "does not have any commits yet" message), so a non-error root repo is handled consistently with the rest of the package.
- **RecentMessages is RAW by design.** The contract is explicit: the prompt layer trims blanks (`sed '/^$/d'`) and caps lines (`head -100`) and runs the multi-line awk scan — log.go returns the raw `---%n%B` stream verbatim and MUST NOT trim it (trimming would corrupt the multi-line-detect algorithm). RecentSubjects, by contrast, IS split + trimmed (it is a plain subject list).
- **Faithful port, no go-git, no shell (PRD §22.3/§19).** Every git call is a `[]string` run via `exec.Command` (the shipped `run` seam); the `--format=---%n%B` / `--format=%s` tokens and the `-<n>` limit are passed as literal args, never interpolated into a shell string — shell injection is structurally impossible. The arg form `--format=---%n%B` (no shell quotes) is verified BYTE-IDENTICAL to the shell `'---%n%B'` form.

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/git/log.go`, FIRST line a PLAIN `package git` (git.go OWNS the `// Package git` doc — do NOT duplicate it; add only a leading file-level comment explaining THIS file adds the three history-query methods over the shipped run seam, mirroring how plumbing.go/diff.go are structured). Imports: stdlib ONLY — `errors`, `fmt`, `strconv`, `strings`. NO go-git, NO testify, NO new dep (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum).
2. **`CommitCount`** — `func (g *Git) CommitCount() (int, error)`:
   - `out, runErr := g.run("rev-list", "--count", "HEAD")`.
   - On a non-zero exit, `errors.As(runErr, &ee)` where `ee *ExitError`; if `ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "unknown revision")` → return `(0, nil)` (the unborn/root repo is the normal root-commit case, NOT a failure — identical detection to plumbing.go's `RevParseHEAD`). Any OTHER non-zero exit (e.g. not-a-git-repo) → return `(0, runErr)` (surface the typed `*ExitError` as-is).
   - On success: `n, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(out))`; on parse error return `(0, fmt.Errorf("git rev-list --count HEAD: parsing %q: %w", out, err))`; else `return n, nil`.
   - godoc cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1 (`commit_count = git rev-list --count HEAD || 0`), external_deps.md §D, and the (0, nil) rootless contract.
3. **`RecentMessages`** — `func (g *Git) RecentMessages(n int) (string, error)`:
   - `out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=---%n%B", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n))`. (The `--format=---%n%B` value is passed as ONE literal arg — `%n`=newline, `%B`=raw body; the `%` is not special in a Go string. Verified byte-identical to the shell `'---%n%B'` form.) The limit is the dash-number form `-<n>` (matches the reference `-20`).
   - On a non-zero exit, `errors.As(runErr, &ee)`; if `ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet")` → return `("", nil)` (unborn repo → empty, NO error — match on "does not have any commits yet", NOT on the branch name which varies main/master/etc.). Any OTHER non-zero exit → return `("", runErr)`.
   - On success: `return out, nil` — the RAW output (trailing newline + embedded blank lines INTACT). Do NOT trim, do NOT cap lines, do NOT strip the `---` separators — the prompt layer does `sed '/^$/d' | head -100` and the multi-line awk scan (reference_impl.md §6). `n <= 0` yields empty stdout (exit 0) naturally; no special guard needed.
   - godoc cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1 + §6, external_deps.md §D, and the RAW (no-trim) + ("", nil) unborn contracts.
4. **`RecentSubjects`** — `func (g *Git) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)`:
   - `out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=%s", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n))`.
   - On a non-zero exit, same unborn detection as RecentMessages (`ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet")`) → return `(nil, nil)`. Any OTHER non-zero exit → return `(nil, runErr)`.
   - On success: split `out` on `"\n"`, `TrimSpace` each element, append non-empty elements to a `[]string`, return it NEWEST-FIRST in order (git log emits newest-first; the trailing newline yields a final empty element that the skip-empties step drops). `n` larger than the commit count returns all subjects (exit 0); `n <= 0` yields empty.
   - godoc cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1, external_deps.md §D, and the (nil, nil) unborn contract.
5. NEW file `internal/git/log_test.go`, `package git` (WHITE-BOX — composes the shipped S2 harness `newTempRepo`/`seedCommits`; mirror plumbing_test.go/diff_test.go's `package git` posture), stdlib `testing` ONLY (import `errors`, `strings`, `testing`). Do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum. Cases (one behavior per `Test*`, driving the REAL git binary git 2.54.0):
   - `TestCommitCount_MatchesSeededCount` — `newTempRepo(t)`; `seedCommits(t, g, []string{...})` of known length N; assert `CommitCount() == (N, nil)`.
   - `TestCommitCount_UnbornReturnsZeroNoError` — `newTempRepo(t)` (unborn); assert `CommitCount() == (0, nil)`.
   - `TestRecentSubjects_NewestFirstInOrder` — `seedCommits(t, g, []string{"sub zero", "sub one", "sub two"})`; assert `RecentSubjects(2)` returns `["sub two", "sub one"]` (newest-first, in order, truncated to the 2 newest) AND `RecentSubjects(50)` returns `["sub two", "sub one", "sub zero"]` (all, newest-first). Each element is trimmed.
   - `TestRecentSubjects_UnbornReturnsEmpty` — `newTempRepo(t)`; assert `RecentSubjects(20)` returns an empty slice (len 0) and a NIL error.
   - `TestRecentMessages_RawFormat` — `seedCommits(t, g, []string{"feat: multi\n\nFirst body line.\nSecond body line.", "fix: single"})`; `got, err := RecentMessages(2)`; `err == nil`; assert `got` Contains the literal `"\n---\n"` separator form (the `---` markers), Contains `"feat: multi"`, Contains `"Second body line."`, and — to PROVE the no-trim contract — Contains a blank line within the body (e.g. the two-consecutive-newline block from the multi-line body). Assert newest-first ordering (the `fix: single` block appears before the `feat: multi` block).
   - `TestRecentMessages_UnbornReturnsEmpty` — `newTempRepo(t)`; assert `RecentMessages(20) == ("", nil)`.
   - NAMING: `TestCommitCount_<Scenario>` / `TestRecentSubjects_<Scenario>` / `TestRecentMessages_<Scenario>`; one behavior per function; use distinctive subject/body strings so assertions are unambiguous.
6. **Scope / boundary**: ONLY create `internal/git/log.go` and `internal/git/log_test.go`. Do NOT modify the shipped git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/plumbing.go/plumbing_test.go/diff.go/diff_test.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, internal/provider. Do NOT run `go mod tidy`. Do NOT implement the sibling stage.go (HasStagedChanges/AddAll — that is T4.S2, a SEPARATE work item). Do NOT implement prompt/examples.go or generate/dedupe.go (later milestones). The `// Package git` doc comment must appear EXACTLY ONCE (in git.go); log.go uses a plain `package git` line.

### Known Gotchas of our codebase & Library Quirks
```go
// CRITICAL (unborn-repo detection has TWO distinct stderr substrings):
//   - `git rev-list --count HEAD` on an unborn repo -> exit 128, stderr 'unknown revision'
//     (IDENTICAL to rev-parse HEAD; reuse plumbing.go RevParseHEAD's detection verbatim for CommitCount).
//   - `git log --format=... -<n>` on an unborn repo -> exit 128, stderr 'does not have any commits yet'
//     (a DIFFERENT message than rev-list/rev-parse; RecentMessages/RecentSubjects match THIS substring,
//      NOT 'unknown revision' and NOT the branch name which varies main/master/etc).
// CRITICAL: RecentMessages returns RAW output (do NOT TrimSpace, do NOT strip the '---' separators,
//   do NOT collapse blank lines). The prompt layer runs sed '/^$/d' | head -100 and the multi-line
//   awk heuristic over the raw '---%n%B' stream; trimming here would corrupt multi-line detection.
// CRITICAL: pass the format as ONE literal arg '--format=---%n%B' (no shell quotes). '%' is NOT special
//   in a Go double-quoted string, so this is the literal value git expands (%n=newline, %B=raw body).
//   Verified byte-identical to the shell '---%n%B' form.
// CRITICAL: the limit is the dash-number form '-<n>' built via fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n) (matches the
//   reference -20 / -50). n<=0 yields empty stdout (exit 0) naturally; no clamp needed.
// CRITICAL: run() is UNEXPORTED -> log.go + log_test.go MUST be white-box package git (NOT git_test).
// git.go OWNS the '// Package git' doc -> log.go uses a PLAIN 'package git' line + a leading file-level comment.
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
package git

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
)

func (g *Git) CommitCount() (int, error)                     { /* rev-list --count HEAD; 0 on unborn */ }
func (g *Git) RecentMessages(n int) (string, error)          { /* log --format=---%n%B -<n>; RAW; "" on unborn */ }
func (g *Git) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)        { /* log --format=%s -<n>; split+trim; nil on unborn */ }
```

Add a leading file-level comment explaining THIS file adds the three history-query methods (CommitCount/RecentMessages/RecentSubjects) over the shipped run seam (mirror plumbing.go's / diff.go's file header).
  - IMPORTS: stdlib ONLY — "errors", "fmt", "strconv", "strings". NO go-git/testify/new dep (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum).

  - IMPLEMENT `func (g *Git) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)`: out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=%s", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n)); if runErr != nil { var ee *ExitError; if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet") { return nil, nil }; return nil, runErr }; var subjects []string; for _, line := range strings.Split(out, "\n") { line = strings.TrimSpace(line); if line != "" { subjects = append(subjects, line) } }; return subjects, nil (newest-first, trimmed). godoc cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1, external_deps.md §D, the (nil,nil) unborn contract.

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details
```go
// CommitCount — unborn detection MIRRORS plumbing.go RevParseHEAD verbatim
// (rev-list --count HEAD emits the SAME 'unknown revision' stderr as rev-parse HEAD).
func (g *Git) CommitCount() (int, error) {
	out, runErr := g.run("rev-list", "--count", "HEAD")
	if runErr != nil {
		var ee *ExitError
		if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "unknown revision") {
			return 0, nil // unborn/root repo — NOT an error (reference: || 0)
		}
		return 0, runErr // genuine failure (e.g. not-a-repo) — surface typed *ExitError
	}
	n, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(out))
	if err != nil {
		return 0, fmt.Errorf("git rev-list --count HEAD: parsing %q: %w", out, err)
	}
	return n, nil
}

// RecentMessages — RAW, no trim. git log on an unborn repo emits a DIFFERENT
// stderr ('does not have any commits yet') than rev-list, so match THAT substring.
func (g *Git) RecentMessages(n int) (string, error) {
	out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=---%n%B", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n))
	if runErr != nil {
		var ee *ExitError
		if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet") {
			return "", nil // unborn repo — NOT an error
		}
		return "", runErr
	}
	return out, nil // RAW — the prompt layer trims+caps; multi-line detect scans this
}

// RecentSubjects — split + trim, newest-first.
func (g *Git) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error) {
	out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=%s", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n))
	if runErr != nil {
		var ee *ExitError
		if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet") {
			return nil, nil // unborn repo — NOT an error
		}
		return nil, runErr
	}
	var subjects []string
	for _, line := range strings.Split(out, "\n") {
		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
		if line != "" {
			subjects = append(subjects, line)
		}
	}
	return subjects, nil
}
```

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on `CommitCount`, `RecentMessages`, and `RecentSubjects` cites PRD §22.3 (shell out to real git, no go-git), §19 (no sh -c), reference_impl.md §1 (the exact history reads: `rev-list --count HEAD || 0`, `git log --format='---%n%B' -20`, `git log --format=%s -50`) — RecentMessages additionally cites §6 (the multi-line awk heuristic that scans the raw examples) — external_deps.md §D (verified git 2.54 commands table), and the unborn-repo non-error contracts ((0,nil) / ("",nil) / (nil,nil)). This is the ONLY documentation this task produces (inline godoc). Do NOT create README.md, docs/, or providers/*.toml here. This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT trim RecentMessages output — return the RAW `---%n%B` stream (the prompt layer does sed '/^$/d' | head -100 and the multi-line awk scan; trimming here corrupts multi-line detection, reference_impl.md §6).
- ❌ Do NOT detect the unborn repo for RecentMessages/RecentSubjects with the 'unknown revision' substring — git log emits 'does not have any commits yet' (a DIFFERENT message). Only CommitCount (rev-list) reuses the 'unknown revision' detection from plumbing.go RevParseHEAD.
- ❌ Do NOT match the unborn-repo branch name (main/master/...) — match the stable substring 'does not have any commits yet'.
- ❌ Do NOT return an error on an unborn repo — return (0,nil) / ("",nil) / (nil,nil) (the root-commit case is normal, reference `|| 0`).
- ❌ Do NOT import go-git/testify/any dep — stdlib only (errors/fmt/strconv/strings); do NOT run `go mod tidy`.
- ❌ Do NOT make log.go/log_test.go `package git_test` — they MUST be white-box `package git` to call the unexported `run` and compose the S2 harness helpers.
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate the `// Package git` doc — it is owned by git.go; use a plain `package git` line + a leading file-level comment.
- ❌ Do NOT build the limit as a shell string or use sh -c — pass `fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n)` as a literal arg and `--format=---%n%B` / `--format=%s` as literal args (PRD §19).
- ❌ Do NOT create stage.go (HasStagedChanges/AddAll) — that is T4.S2, a SEPARATE work item.
- ❌ Do NOT touch git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/plumbing*.go/diff*.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, internal/provider.
- ❌ Do NOT trim subjects with anything that collapses internal spacing — `strings.TrimSpace` each line only (subjects are one line each from %s).

Add a godoc citing PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1 (`commit_count = git rev-list --count HEAD || 0`), external_deps.md §D, and the (0,nil) rootless contract.

Implement `func (g *Git) RecentMessages(n int) (string, error)`: `out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=---%n%B", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n))` (the `--format=---%n%B` value is ONE literal arg — `%n`=newline, `%B`=raw body; `%` is not special in a Go string, and this arg form is verified byte-identical to the shell `'---%n%B'` form; the limit is the dash-number form `-<n>` matching the reference `-20`); if runErr != nil { `var ee *ExitError`; `if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet") { return "", nil }` (unborn repo → empty, NOT an error — match the stable substring, NOT the branch name which varies main/master); otherwise `return "", runErr` }; on success `return out, nil` — the RAW output with trailing newline + embedded blank lines INTACT. Do NOT trim, do NOT cap lines, do NOT strip the `---` separators. Add a godoc citing PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1 + §6, external_deps.md §D, the RAW no-trim contract, and the ("",nil) unborn contract.

Implement `func (g *Git) RecentSubjects(n int) ([]string, error)`: `out, runErr := g.run("log", "--format=%s", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n))`; if runErr != nil { `var ee *ExitError`; `if errors.As(runErr, &ee) && ee.Code == 128 && strings.Contains(ee.Stderr, "does not have any commits yet") { return nil, nil }`; otherwise `return nil, runErr` }; on success split `out` on "\n", `strings.TrimSpace` each element, append non-empty elements to a `[]string`, and return it NEWEST-FIRST in order (git log emits newest-first; the trailing newline yields a final empty element the skip-empties step drops). Add a godoc citing PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1, external_deps.md §D, and the (nil,nil) unborn contract.

Use the stdlib `testing` package ONLY (import `errors`, `strings`, `testing`); do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum. Implement ONE behavior per Test* function, driving the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0).

CommitCount runs `git rev-list --count HEAD`; on an unborn/rootless repo (exit 128, stderr containing 'unknown revision') it returns (0, nil) — NOT an error — mirroring plumbing.go RevParseHEAD's detection verbatim; on success it returns strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(out)); any OTHER non-zero exit surfaces the typed *ExitError.

RecentMessages runs `git log --format=---%n%B -<n>` (the --format=---%n%B passed as ONE literal arg, the limit as fmt.Sprintf("-%d", n)) and returns the RAW stdout VERBATIM (no trimming, no line capping, no '---' stripping) — the prompt layer does sed '/^$/d' | head -100 and the multi-line awk scan; on an unborn repo (exit 128, stderr containing 'does not have any commits yet') it returns ("", nil) — NOT an error; any OTHER non-zero exit surfaces the typed *ExitError.

RecentSubjects runs `git log --format=%s -<n>`, splits stdout on "\n", TrimSpaces each element, drops empties, and returns the []string NEWEST-FIRST in order; on an unborn repo (same 'does not have any commits yet' detection) it returns (nil, nil) — NOT an error; any OTHER non-zero exit surfaces the typed *ExitError.
